name: AMEREN CORP
business: Ameren Corporation is a public utility holding company. Through its subsidiaries, it operates rate-regulated electric generation, transmission, and distribution businesses and a rate-regulated natural gas distribution business in Missouri and Illinois. It also operates a FERC rate-regulated electric transmission business in the MISO.

exposures.sentence: In February 2026, Ameren Missouri executed electric service agreements with large load customers under the large load customer rate plan, representing 2.2 gigawatts of demand.

The 2025 Change to the 2023 PRP includes, among other things, the following: ... adding 1,000 MWs of battery storage by 2030, which includes the Big Hollow Battery Energy Storage Project discussed in Note 2 Rate and Regulatory Matters under Part II, Item 8, of this report.
The 2025 Change to the 2023 PRP includes, among other things, the following: ... adding 1,500 MWs of nuclear generation by 2040;

The 2025 Change to the 2023 PRP includes, among other things, the following: ... adding 3,200 MWs of renewable generation by 2030, which includes the solar generation projects discussed in Note 2 Rate and Regulatory Matters under Part II, Item 8, of this report, and an additional 1,500 MWs by 2035;
ATXI operates a FERC rate-regulated electric transmission business in the MISO.

The Grid Plan outlines how Ameren Illinois expects to invest in electric distribution infrastructure in order to support grid modernization, clean energy, energy efficiency, and the state of Illinois renewable energy, equity, climate, electrification, and environmental goals.

catalysts.event: MoPSC order approving $355 million electric rate increase for Ameren Missouri effective June 1, 2025
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, the MoPSC issued an order in Ameren Missouri's 2024 electric service regulatory rate review, approving nonunanimous stipulations and agreements. The order authorized an increase of $355 million to Ameren Missouri's annual revenue requirement for electric retail service, effective June 1, 2025.

catalysts.event: MoPSC order approving $32 million natural gas rate increase for Ameren Missouri effective September 1, 2025
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, the MoPSC issued an order in Ameren Missouri's 2024 natural gas delivery service regulatory rate review, approving a unanimous stipulation and agreement. The order authorized an increase of $32 million to Ameren Missouri's annual revenue requirement for natural gas delivery service, effective September 1, 2025.

catalysts.event: ICC order approving $79 million natural gas rate increase for Ameren Illinois effective December 2025
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, the ICC issued an order in Ameren Illinois January 2025 natural gas delivery service regulatory rate review, which resulted in an increase to its annual revenues for natural gas delivery service of $79 million based on a 9.60% ROE, a capital structure composed of 50% common equity, a 2026 future test year, and a rate base of $3.2 billion.

catalysts.event: Execution of electric service agreements with large load customers representing 2.2 GW of demand
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, Ameren Missouri executed electric service agreements with large load customers under its large load customer rate plan, representing 2.2 gigawatts of demand.

catalysts.event: MoPSC approval of CCN for Big Hollow Natural Gas (800-MW) and Big Hollow Battery Energy Storage (400-MW) projects
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, the MoPSC issued an order approving a nonunanimous stipulation and agreement related to a requested CCN for the Big Hollow Natural Gas (800-MW facility) and the Big Hollow Battery Energy Storage (400-MW facility) projects.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of Split Rail Solar Project expected in-service Q2 2026
catalysts.sentence: Also in February 2026, Ameren Missouri acquired the Split Rail Solar Project, which includes solar panels, project design, land rights, and engineering, procurement, and construction agreements, for approximately $600 million, and took over construction management of the project, which is expected to be placed in-service in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Expected filing of next preferred resource plan
catalysts.sentence: Ameren Missouri expects to file its next preferred resource plan in September 2026.

catalysts.event: Next scheduled refueling of Callaway Energy Center
catalysts.sentence: The next refueling is scheduled for the fall of 2026.
In January 2026, the CRGA was enacted and will become effective in June 2026.
